title: Decision
description: A DEC-* record: a single architectural or product decision tied to a spec and a requirement. Decisions name the why; specs name the what. Every DEC must enumerate the alternatives, the rationale, the evidence consulted, and the rollback path.

spec.description: Path to the owning spec ledger directory, relative to the repo root. Example: specs/0001-cognitive-delivery-control-plane/.

requirement.description: Identifier of the requirement this decision resolves. Example: R-AFB-0001-7 or a free-form requirement label used by the spec.

date.description: ISO 8601 date when the decision was recorded (YYYY-MM-DD).

status.description: Lifecycle state. A decision starts proposed, becomes approved once merged, becomes reversed if rolled back, or superseded if replaced by a later DEC.

reversible.description: True if the decision can be undone with bounded effort (config flag, single-file revert). False if it carries lock-in (data migration, public API change, vendor commitment).

decision.description: One- or two-sentence statement of what was chosen.

alternatives.description: Other paths considered. Each entry names the option and why it was rejected. Empty arrays are not allowed: if there were no alternatives, this should not be a DEC.

label.description: Short name for the alternative path.

rejected_because.description: Concrete reason the alternative was not chosen. Avoid vague rationale.

rationale.description: Why the chosen path beats the alternatives, in the author's own words.

evidence.description: Pointers to the artifacts, runs, benchmarks, or prior decisions consulted. Each entry has a kind tag and an opaque ref (URL, path, run id).

kind.description: Evidence category. Common values: spec, run, benchmark, postmortem, decision, doc, screenshot, log, trace.

ref.description: Pointer to the evidence. Path, URL, or stable identifier.

rollback.description: Concrete steps to undo this decision, written so a future maintainer can execute them without context.

owner.description: Person or guild accountable for the decision.

amends.description: ID of a prior DEC that this DEC amends. The prior DEC is not changed; this DEC documents the amendment as a sibling artifact.

supersedes.description: ID of a prior DEC that this DEC supersedes. Use sparingly — supersession is irreversible decision history.

superseded_by.description: Id of a newer DEC that replaces this one. Set when status becomes superseded.

systems_map.description: What underlying system or mechanism does this decision expose or change? Names the systemic pattern, not just the local concern. Recommended for any DEC with Status: approved; see DEC-CDCP-020 for the discipline.

transferable_principle.description: What generalizes beyond this specific decision? Names the principle that applies in other contexts or repos. Recommended for any DEC with Status: approved.

falsification_test.description: What observation or experiment would prove this decision wrong? Names the empirical condition that would invalidate the decision. Recommended for any DEC with Status: approved.

adoption_ladder.description: Stepwise adoption from minimum viable to full, with monitoring signals at each step. Recommended for any DEC with Status: approved.
